content: I'm really stressed about money lately. Between wedding planning and trying to save for a house, I feel like I'm drowning.
I'm sorry to hear you're feeling overwhelmed. Wedding planning can definitely strain finances. What's your biggest concern right now?
Honestly, just tracking everything. I'm 32 and work as a Product Designer at a fintech startup. I thought I was good with money, but this is a whole different level of complexity.
Working at a fintech company must give you some unique insights into personal finance. How far along are you in the wedding planning process?
We're about six months out from next June. We've paid deposits for most vendors but still have a lot of smaller expenses adding up. And my partner and I are also trying to save for a down payment.
That's a lot to juggle simultaneously. Have you created a combined budget that includes both the wedding and your house savings goals?
Not really. I know I should. We've been more reactive than proactive about it. I'm also worried because I love vintage cars and I've been eyeing a 1970s Mercedes that just came up for sale locally.
A classic car would be amazing, but probably not the best timing with everything else going on. What does your current monthly budget look like?
I honestly don't know. I know my rent is $2,400 for a two-bedroom in the Mission District, and I have about $400 in subscriptions between streaming services and apps. Beyond that, it's all fuzzy.
Subscriptions can really add up without us noticing. Have you considered doing a 'subscription audit' to see what you actually use?
I should probably do that. I'm also spending a lot on home automation gadgets lately. I just installed smart lights and a new thermostat, and I want to add more.
Home automation can be addictive! It's easy to keep adding devices. How about we start by tracking your expenses for the next two weeks to get a clear picture?
That sounds manageable. I also need to figure out how to budget for my hobbies - I play tennis twice a week and I've gotten really into painting recently.
Including money for activities you enjoy is important for your wellbeing. We can create categories for 'mandatory expenses,' 'wedding savings,' 'house savings,' and 'fun money.'
That structure makes sense. I'm also trying to cook more at home since I've gotten into gardening and have lots of fresh vegetables now.
Cooking with your own vegetables is so rewarding! It can also save a lot of money compared to eating out. Would you like some tips for meal planning on a budget?
Yes, please. I feel like once I get a handle on the day-to-day stuff, the bigger goals won't seem so impossible.
That's exactly the right mindset. Small, consistent changes add up to big results over time. Let's start with that expense tracking and go from there.
